skill.name: writing-beats
skill.description: Shape an article as a journey of beats, choose-your-own-adventure style. The user picks a starting beat from the raw material, you write only that beat, then offer options for where to pivot next, beat by beat, until the article reaches a natural end. Use when the user has raw material and wants to assemble it as a narrative rather than an argument.
skill.summary: Guide article drafting one narrative beat at a time from raw markdown material.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptography pattern at SKILL.md line 3 and a system reconnaissance pattern at line 10. Both are false positives in context: the file contains writing workflow instructions, not executable code, cryptography, host inspection, networking, or credential access.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged location is the skill description for a beat-based writing workflow. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, encryption library, or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter prose describing article structure. I found no evidence of cryptographic behavior at this location.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged location asks the assistant to request a user-selected save path for the article. It does not instruct host enumeration, environment discovery, command execution, or unauthorized file search.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is limited to asking the user where to save writing output. I found no evidence of system reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Raw notes often become flat articles when every idea is used at once. This skill turns source material into an interactive beat-by-beat writing process.
actual_capabilities: Turns raw markdown material into candidate starting beats.
Writes only the beat selected by the user.
Offers two or three next-beat options after each draft step.
Re-reads the article file before each write to preserve user edits.
Supports rewriting or replacing a previous beat when requested.
limitations: Requires raw material from the user before the workflow is useful.
Depends on user choices between beats at each step.
Does not draft a complete article in one automatic pass.
Does not verify facts beyond the supplied material.

use_cases.title: Turn Research Notes Into Narrative
use_cases.description: Convert a loose markdown file into a guided article with deliberate sequence and pacing.

use_cases.title: Develop Technical Blog Posts
use_cases.description: Build a post from examples, observations, and fragments without forcing a rigid outline first.

use_cases.title: Shape Founder Essays
use_cases.description: Test different openings and pivots while keeping each draft move small and reviewable.

prompt_templates.title: Start From Raw Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a new article from a markdown file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats with my raw notes file. Ask where to save the article, then show me starting beat options.

prompt_templates.title: Continue An Existing Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Resume a beat-by-beat article already in progress.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue this article using writing-beats. Re-read the draft first, then suggest the next beat choices.

prompt_templates.title: Try A Different Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore alternative structure after a draft starts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats to offer three different next beats: one personal, one practical, and one surprising.

prompt_templates.title: Revise A Previous Beat
prompt_templates.scenario: Replace a specific beat without rewriting the whole article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Go back to beat 3 and suggest alternative versions. After I choose one, edit that beat in place and preserve the rest.

output_examples.input: A markdown file with scattered notes about launching a new developer tool.
output_examples.output: The assistant presents three possible openings, such as a failed first attempt, a user quote, or a technical constraint.

output_examples.input: A partial article where the first two beats are already written.
output_examples.output: The assistant re-reads the draft and proposes next moves that could deepen the story, shift to evidence, or introduce tension.

output_examples.input: A request to rewrite beat 3 after the article feels too abstract.
output_examples.output: The assistant offers concrete replacement beats and updates only the chosen section after the user decides.

best_practices: Provide raw material with examples, quotes, observations, and unused fragments.
Choose beats based on the reader journey, not only topic coverage.
Review each beat before asking for the next one.
anti_patterns: Asking for a complete article in one pass.
Using it without enough source material to draw from.
Approving every suggested beat without checking the article direction.

faq.question: What does this skill mean by a beat?
faq.answer: A beat is one small move in the article, such as a scene, point, question, example, or pivot.

faq.question: Does it write the full article at once?
faq.answer: No. It writes one selected beat at a time, then asks what direction to take next.

faq.question: Can it use an existing draft?
faq.answer: Yes. It can re-read the draft and continue from the current article state.

faq.question: Can I change an earlier beat?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports rewriting or replacing a previous beat while preserving the rest.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: A markdown file with raw notes, examples, anecdotes, claims, quotes, and leftover fragments works best.

faq.question: Is this mainly for arguments or stories?
faq.answer: It is best for narrative articles where the order of discovery matters to the reader.
